observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
expedite the retrieval of all JEVs, general ledgers, and all other necessary reports and documents relating to the Trust Fund transactions and prepare JEVs to correct the erroneous entries; and
submit to the Audit Team the revised CY 2024 pre-closing trial balances and Statement of Cash Flows for the Trust Fund books, including the JEVs, general ledgers, and the complete set of the revised CY 2024 financial statements, for further evaluation.

We recommended and Management agreed through the concerned office to:
initiate steps to speed up the implementation of automation of processes in relation to RPT/SET accounts in the MTO with the end in view of updating of all RPTARs or Taxpayer’s Index Cards and the certified list of taxpayers starting CY 2025; and
ensure that accruals on real property and special education taxes starting CY 2025 be based on the certified list of taxpayers and RPTARs or Taxpayer’s Index Cards as of the start of the calendar year.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare and maintain a Lapsing Schedule for all depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment. Ensure completeness of all PPE items in the Lapsing Schedule; and
recompute for the accumulated depreciation balances for each PPE item as of January 1, 2024 and 2025 and make JEVs to reflect the accurate balances, taking into consideration the pertinent provisions in the computation of depreciation.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant used the declining balance method because there has been no lapsing schedule turned over from the previous accountant. This might be the reason why the total depreciation expenses for CY 2024 alone is equivalent to 49.14 per cent of the total accumulated depreciation as of December 31, 2024.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
create and maintain special accounts and complete subsidiary ledgers for the 20 per cent Development Fund and all economic enterprises starting CY 2025 and ensure that all assets, liabilities, income and expenses for each special account be separately accounted for, prepared with complete sets of books of accounts, general and subsidiary ledgers, and separate financial statements; and
ensure that the CY 2025 financial statements include disclosures on segment information as required in IPSAS 18 and COA Circular No. 2023-007.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
submit a written justification, including other documents or records requested, to the Audit Team on the above-cited deficiencies for further evaluation;
conduct a thorough records/documents tracing, as far as practicable, on the Land account totaling ₱8,008,370.00 with the end view of identifying the specific parcels of land owned by the Municipality and for the preparation and maintenance of subsidiary ledgers; and
expedite the registration of the parcels of land identified to be owned by the Municipality of Ronda under the Torrens Title System.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to release the share of RPT to component barangays within the period as prescribed in Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160 in order for the latter to utilize the funds for its priority projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
maintain the PCFs under the imprest system. Make sure that no movements shall be made in the Petty Cash account balance other than its initial setup during the year and the full liquidation at the end of the year pursuant to Section 48 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
ensure that all PCFs be fully liquidated starting CY 2025, and that the corresponding unutilized portion of the fund be issued with official receipts; and
prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Petty Cash account pursuant to Section 111 of PD No. 1445 and Section 4.e of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and Management through the concerned office agreed to:
enforce compliance with the timely submission of payroll documents, such as DTRs and Accomplishment Reports, among others, by all the Plantilla and JO personnel immediately after every end of each payroll period with the end in view of ascertaining the validity and regularity of supporting documents and the accuracy of the total net amount to drawn in the payroll cash advances; and
ensure that all payroll fund supporting documents as required in Item 1.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 are duly attached in the disbursement vouchers instead of in the liquidation reports.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant who agreed to send demand letters requiring the accountable officer to immediately render full liquidation of their respective cash advances, otherwise, implement the applicable remedy such as initiate the filing of appropriate administrative and criminal proceedings against them.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Audit Team the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents on the above transaction, including a written justification on the above-cited deficiency and additional documents such as a declaration of such property as an important cultural property from the appropriate cultural agency, for further evaluation by the Audit Team.

We recommended and Management through the concerned office agreed to secure from the NTC the required Purchaser’s Permit and follow-up from the supplier the requested copy of the Permit to Sell to consider the procurement valid and legitimate expenditures to prevent disallowance in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
issue an executive order for the formation of Fire Brigades on all municipal-owned buildings and structures that can accommodate at least 50 persons in compliance with Division 2 of the 2019 Revised IRR of RA No. 9514;
ensure that the members of Fire Brigades undergo trainings and practice drills on fire prevention, fire suppression, rescue, emergency medical services and related emergency response in compliance with Divisions 3 and 4 of the 2019 Revised IRR of RA No. 9514; and
coordinate with the Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP) to conduct a comprehensive inspection on all municipal-owned buildings, structures and facilities in order to identify non-compliance and potential fire hazards, implement appropriate measures to mitigate them effectively, and ensure compliance with issuance of appropriate documents, reports and certificates such as FSICs, among others.

We recommended and the Chairperson of the Municipal School Board and the Co-Chairperson as well as the LSB members concerned agreed to ensure that the submission of the quarterly and annual SEF utilization reports to the Sangguniang Bayan, the DepEd Central Office, DBM Regional 7 be made within the reglementary period in addition to their submission to the DILG in compliance with Section 6.1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 2017.

We recommended and Management through the concerned official agreed to submit a written justification to the Audit Team on the above-noted deficiencies and cause the revision of EO No. 34, series of 2022 taken into consideration the noted deficiencies in the composition of the GFPS, TWG, Secretariat and non-creation of the GAD M&E Team, with the end in view of achieving full compliance with Section 4.1.A of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed through the:
GAD Focal Person to confer with the PCW, DILG, or other government authorities on the availability of capability trainings such as HGDG, among others, as provided in Item 4.1(A)(3) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended and ensure that all elected officials and department heads to attend with the end in view of applying such capabilities towards the implementation of the Magna Carta of Women; and
Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate sufficient funds for capability building programs on GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE as of December 31, 2020, render the report thereon and provide a copy of the report to the audit team pursuant to Section 266 of the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that Management provide the audit team with the basis of the Municipality in granting honoraria to the members of the Municipal Inspection Team such as Executive Order creating the Team stating the conditions and functions of the members with stipulations to receive honoraria, Sangguniang Bayan Authority and any other documents to establish the validity of the claims.

We recommended that the concerned Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the November and December 2020 official receipts, disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and other reports due for submission.

We recommended that the KALAHI-CIDSS Municipal Inter-Agency Committee be required to submit: (a) order creating the committee; (b) duly approved special project plan indicating the nature of work assigned to the local government officials and employees, duties and responsibilities and extent of their participation; and (c) accomplishment reports which states the man-hours spent as well as the completed deliverables pursuant to DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2.

We recommended that Management direct those responsible for the procurement to submit a written justification for the noted over-priced medical supplies/equipment.  We also recommended that Management, henceforth,  be guided by the prices of Essential Emergency Medicines and Medical Devices set by DOH during this COVID-19 Health Crisis to ensure that the government achieves the best value for money in sourcing essential medical goods.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation on what have transpired during the process and make representation to the DSWD as to the proper action on this matter. Furthermore, Management send demand letters to the ineligible beneficiaries for the refund of the amount received totaling ₱3,054,000.00 and ensure that these disbursed funds are collected and returned to DSWD.
We recommended that Management direct the Property Officer to prepare the PAR instead of ARE in the issuances of property, plant and equipment in accordance with Section 259 of GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit explanation/justification on non-compliance with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.
Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC always be updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid non-compliance in the future.

We recommended that in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, the Municipality conduct Pre-Inspection on the damaged properties and Post-Inspection of the work accomplished, prepare and submit Post and Pre-Repair Inspection Reports, Report of Waste Materials and Property Index Card summarizing all the repairs and maintenance performed on each service vehicle.

We recommended that Management adhere to Section 22 of Omnibus Rules on Leave, Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and rules and regulations related thereto in the payment of monetization of accumulated leave credits.

We recommended that Management provide an explanation/justification regarding the breach of enhanced community quarantine protocols by allowing all employees and workers to physically report to work and failing to formulate and implement alternative work arrangements.  Likewise, we recommended that Management submit the Office Order by the Head of Agency or approved alternative work arrangement requiring the employees and workers to report to their workstations during the ECQ period.

We recommended that Management require the concerned official to prepare and post the reports as required under Section 4 of DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020. We also recommended that the Department Heads must be aware of whatever guidelines intended to them in order to ensure proper implementation thereof.

We recommended that Management submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances as of December 31, 2020 together with the necessary supporting documents and henceforth, adhere to the provisions and reportorial requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 to ensure that aids and donations received by the Municipality are properly accounted for.
We recommended that Management comply with Section 8 of RA 11313 and Section 9 of its IRR to ensure enforcement of the provisions of the Safe Spaces Act.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to submit the year-end financial reports on or before the prescribed deadline.  Otherwise, cause the suspension of payment of salaries of personnel concerned pursuant to Section 122 (2) of P.D. 1445.

We further recommended that Management, in consultation with the accounting personnel, identify the cause/s of delay in finalizing the year-end reports to come up with suitable and workable solution to this perennial problem of the Municipality and thus facilitate that financial statements are submitted on or before the deadline.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the former Municipal Mayor , Budget Officer, accountant and Treasurer should submit their explanation on the expenditures charged against the Kilos Asenso Fund which were not related to the  development/rehabilitation of the Banlot Spring Waterworks system. Likewise, a Sangguniang  Bayan Resolution on the realignment of the  said fund to MOOE should be submitted, otherwise, penal sanctions pursuant to Article 220of the Revised Penal Code shall be imposed upon the persons held liable.  Also, the municipal engineer should submit the Plans and Programs and Estimates (POWE) of the project and the physical report as of December 31, 2009.  A report on the status of the project and utilization of the fund reported to the DBM should also be submitted. Likewise, the Human Resource Officer should submit copies of the Job Order for labor services, not attached to the  payrolls, for our verification if they were related to rehabilitation /development of the Banlot Spring.  Also, the Municipal Mayor should follow strictly Section 5(5.8) of COA Circular No. 97-002 and liquidate immediately his outstanding cash advances amounting to P175,000.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Budget Officer, the one million funds intended for Kilos Asenso was partially spent for MOOE which amounted to P614,70.34 because some projects implemented by Mayor Esteban Sia did not have its own appropriation.  Whatever present funds were available, he can remember he easily said charged to Kilos Asenso.  I was really aware in the first place that juggling of funds is not good but because I am afraid to lose my job, I was forced to obey to his longings.  We were always threatened.

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Budget Officer, despite her refusal to sign the ALOBS, but because she was always threatened, she had nothing to do but obey to their demands.  She was just a poor employee against them who have money and in power.

And SB resolution authorizing the former Municipal Mayor to enter into contract the choreographers and Ms. Maria Shiela Ngo of Kalye Musika should also be submitted.
